_comment: Scenario 33: PLANNER + AVOIDER validation with HD-map obstacles and ground-truth simulated perception (Cosys-AirSim). Replaces the previous PATH A perception-stress role (now scenario 34, see issue #698). Inputs: (a) static_obstacles populated from tools/diag/blocks_default_inventory.json so the planner has a complete obstacle map; (b) all perception backends swapped to Cosys ground-truth (depth_estimator=cosys_airsim, sam.backend=cosys_airsim, radar.backend=cosys_airsim_groundtruth) so any perception output is pixel/pose perfect. With both inputs trustworthy, this scenario isolates planner+avoider+control-loop behaviour: D* Lite must detour around a 20×20m solid block to reach WP3 at (40, 14), navigate east leg, return home. Pass = mission complete, zero obstacle collisions.

gt_class_map._comment: Ground-truth class map. Non-COCO obstacles use custom id 100 (mapped to GEOMETRIC_OBSTACLE by PATH A). Person uses COCO class 0.

scenario.name: planner_hdmap_groundtruth_perception
scenario.description: Planner+avoider validation with HD-map static_obstacles populated from the live Cosys-AirSim scene inventory and ground-truth perception for all sensors (depth, segmentation, radar). Drone must complete a 6-waypoint mission that requires detouring around a 20x20m solid cube block. Isolates planner/avoider/control behaviour from perception completeness — see scenario 34 for the perception-only counterpart.
scenario._comment_base_config: Hint for tests/run_scenario_cosys.sh — used when caller does not pass --base-config. Makes `./tests/run_scenario_cosys.sh config/scenarios/33_non_coco_obstacles.json --gui` work without any extra flags.
scenario._comment_scene: Issue #698 — switched from cosys_static.json (9 spawned objects) to cosys_empty.json (no spawned objects).  The Blocks default UE5 scene's TemplateCubes (Cube_7/9/62/66 at known positions per collision-log mining) serve as the obstacle field.  Waypoints are designed around those cubes; spawning more obstacles on top would close the corridors we need.

perception._comment_detector: Perception v2 (Epic #520) design: detector provides class labels only — SAM masks are the primary obstacle source. Switched from color_contour (ground-texture ghost cells) to YOLOv8 so the detector fires ONLY on real COCO objects (person, chair, couch, bush). Non-COCO obstacles (wall, pillar, cube) have no detector bbox, get GEOMETRIC_OBSTACLE via MaskClassAssigner fallback, and are represented in the grid purely through PATH A voxels. Detector output also drives dynamic TTL cells for reactive avoidance of moving objects — static promotion is paused when voxel_input is enabled (see mission_planner.occupancy_grid.voxel_input).

depth_estimator._comment: Issue #698 diagnostic — switched DA V2 → cosys_airsim ground-truth depth (one-run experiment).  DA V2 was producing noise voxels everywhere (mega-cluster id=8 covering 99.5% of voxels regardless of cluster.eps_m setting) because every projected pixel got a depth value, real or hallucinated.  cosys_airsim depth comes straight from UE5's depth buffer — no ML inference, no noise on textureless surfaces.  If voxels now cluster cleanly on real cubes only, DA V2 was the dominant noise source and we know to either (a) stay on cosys_airsim depth for sim testing, (b) add stronger DA V2 noise filters for real-hardware path, or (c) replace DA V2 with stereo depth from the existing P3 stereo cameras.  REVERT to depth_anything_v2 once experiment is logged.  Original DA V2 settings preserved as comments below.

semantic_projector._comment_sample_grid_size: Issue #698 — reduced 16 → 6 (run 2026-05-04_213343_ABORTED showed 648 promoted phantom cells around the drone after eps_m=0.1 still produced one mega-cluster). 16×16=256 probes per SAM mask × ~37 masks/frame × 408 frames = ~3.9M voxels, density ~88 voxels/m³ across the 50×50×6 m volume. At that density EVERY voxel has ~270 neighbours within 0.9 m so spatial clustering chains across the whole field regardless of eps_m. 6×6=36 probes drops voxel count to 14% (~550K total), density ~12/m³ — sparse enough that real-cube voxels (densely packed on each cube face) cluster as one component while depth-noise stays as small components rejected by min_pts=5. Real cube faces still get >36 probes because masks for nearby large objects span many bbox px; only tiny (background) noise masks lose probes proportionally.
semantic_projector._comment_texture_gate_threshold: Issue #698 — enabled (was 0.0 = disabled).  Texture gate rejects depth samples in low-gradient regions of the depth map — exactly where DA V2 noise lives (textureless walls, sky, flat surfaces).  Real cube edges have high depth gradients (neighbour pixels differ by metres) and survive.  0.5 is a documented safe starting value for DA V2 @ 518×518 on Blocks; tighten if more noise leaks through, loosen if real cubes are missed.
semantic_projector._comment_max_depth_saturation_band_m: Issue #698 — DA V2 saturates at max_depth_m via NaN/edge-clamp/degenerate-fit code paths in convert_raw_to_metric.  Pre-#698 the projector accepted those saturated samples as legitimate horizon-distance obstacles, seeding 4596 ghost cells in run 2026-05-02_161132_ABORTED that walled off WP2.  Reject anything within 0.5 m of the configured max as 'I don't know, defaulted to far'.  Scenario default sufficient at 0.5 m; tighten to 0.1 if false negatives on real horizon obstacles ever appear.

path_a._comment: PATH A enabled with real class-agnostic SAM via FastSAM ONNX (YOLOv8-seg architecture trained on SA-1B). Produces masks for every distinct object/surface in the frame regardless of COCO class. Prerequisite: run `bash models/download_fastsam.sh` once to fetch FastSAM-s.pt and export to models/fastsam_s.onnx (~50 MB, ~80 ms/frame CPU). MaskDepthProjector back-projects the masks via Depth Anything V2 into world-frame voxels; non-COCO obstacles (walls, pillars, TemplateCubes) get GEOMETRIC_OBSTACLE labels because they don't match any YOLOv8 detector bbox.

sam._comment: Issue #698 — switched fastsam → cosys_airsim (sim-only ground-truth instance segmentation).  CosysSegmentationBackend pulls ImageType::Segmentation from UE5 and emits one InferenceDetection per visible scene object with pixel-perfect masks.  Eliminates SAM-mega-mask collapse + DA-V2-noise-into-flawed-masks.  Pair with depth_estimator.backend=cosys_airsim for end-to-end ground-truth PATH A.  REVERT to fastsam to validate against real-hardware code path.  Original fastsam settings preserved as _fastsam_* keys for easy revert.
sam._comment_include: Allowlist mode — only objects whose names contain TemplateCube, Cube, Wall, Pillar, Couch, or Bush become obstacles. Run 2026-05-04_220346_ABORTED's first init log showed Cosys returns 273 named objects + 2.7M color slots; with the allowlist we're explicit about which named objects matter and don't depend on a complete blocklist.

altitude_filter._comment: Issue #645 (#658) — drop voxels above max_z_m (sky misprojections from SAM masking distant background) and below min_z_m (ground patches with depth bias).  Run 2026-04-30_174147 trace analysis: 22% of voxels were above 6 m and 10% below 0.3 m — a guaranteed 32% of every frame was ghost.  World-frame Z; thresholds 0 = disabled.  Mission area obstacles top out at 5 m (pillars), drone flies at 5 m altitude, so anything above 6 m is sky.

mask_size_filter._comment: Issue #645 (#659) — drop SAM masks whose bbox area falls outside [min_area_px, max_area_px] BEFORE back-projection.  Run 2026-04-30_174147: SAM produced 41-47 masks per 1280×720 frame for ~7 spawned obstacles.  Tiny masks (<900 px ≈ 30×30) are texture features that produce scattered ghost voxels.  Huge masks (>200,000 px ≈ 22% of frame) are sky / ground regions that produce ghost walls.  Real obstacles in this scenario produce masks of ~1k-50k px depending on distance.  0 = disable bound.

diag._comment: Issue #612 voxel-position trace — JSONL of pose + bboxes + voxel world positions per batch. Consumed by tools/plot_voxel_trace.py. The runner overwrites trace_path with <scenario_log_dir>/path_a_voxel_trace.jsonl so every run is self-contained; keep trace_voxels=true to opt in.

cluster._comment: Issue #698 — eps_m progressively tightened 1.0 → 0.3 → 0.1 (run 2026-05-04_194349_ABORTED still showed 99.7% of voxels in inst=8 at eps=0.3).  Architectural root cause: cpu_semantic_projector.h's project_masked/project_bbox_centre never sets VoxelUpdate.instance_id, so every voxel arrives at the clusterer as instance_id=0 and the clusterer must reconstruct mask membership purely from spatial proximity.  Dense cube-surface voxels chain transitively into adjacent depth-noise voxels and the whole field collapses to one mega-cluster.  At eps_m=0.1 the chain radius drops to ~0.3 m, which is below typical phantom-voxel spacing.  Real-obstacle clusters with dense (<0.3 m) sampling stay merged; sparse depth-noise stays as small components and gets rejected by min_pts=5.  If even this still produces a mega-cluster, the projector needs the architecturally correct fix (propagate per-mask instance_id from SAM into VoxelUpdate).

tracker._comment: Issue #638 Phase 2 — cross-frame instance tracker. max_match_distance_m=3.0 covers per-frame cluster motion at scenario-33 cruise (drone @2 m/s × 100 ms tick + depth jitter). track_max_age_s=2.0 keeps tracks alive through brief occlusions (yaw past pillar) but drops stale ghosts.

perception._comment_radar: Issue #698 — switched perception.radar.backend cosys_airsim → cosys_airsim_groundtruth.  CosysGroundTruthRadarBackend queries simListInstanceSegmentationPoses() instead of doing lidar-emulation cluster binning, so every emitted detection is a real visible scene object with ground-truth (range, azimuth, elevation, radial-velocity).  No ground bounce, no multipath, no regular-grid clutter pattern (run 2026-05-04_220913 planner_grid_overlay showed 68 cyan radar cells in a 2 m-spaced grid pattern around the drone — pure HAL clustering noise).  include_substrings is the SAME allowlist used by CosysSegmentationBackend so radar and camera report the same set of obstacles.  REVERT to cosys_airsim to validate against the lidar-emulation code path.

slam._pose_source_comment: Issue #696 workaround — VIO pipeline froze mid-scenario in run 2026-05-02_153630_ABORTED at (10.22, 20.13) with feat=-1 matches=-1 imu=0; downstream went stale-blind. Until the underlying VIO freeze is debugged, P3 reads ground-truth pose directly from Cosys-AirSim (sim only).

occupancy_grid._comment_inflation_radius_m: Issue #645 — was 2.5 m, which created a closed ring of overlapping halos around the spawned obstacle cluster (pillar_01/wall_01/wall_02/couch_01/pillar_02 at coords (8-22, 10-14)).  Pairwise-gap analysis on the cluster: pillar_01↔wall = 0.03 m, wall↔couch = -2.6 m, wall↔bush = -0.4 m, pillar_02↔couch = -3.4 m — FOUR pairs fully blocked, no path WP2→WP3 through the cluster.  D* Lite gave up 108 times in run 2026-04-30_170604; drone hovered at WP2 and timed out to LOITER.  Reducing to 1.5 m reopens four gaps (pillar_01↔wall = 2.0 m, wall↔bush = 1.6 m, pillar_02↔wall = 2.8 m, pillar_01↔chair = 4.5 m) while still blocking the genuinely-too-close pair wall↔couch (5.4 m apart, will always block at any reasonable inflation — drone must go AROUND not through).  Drone is 0.6 m wide so a 1.5 m halo on a 0.5-m-radius obstacle gives 1 m of effective clearance, well above safety margin.  Issue #698 — further reduced 1.5 → 1.0 m because the TemplateCube_Rounded_7 (a default Blocks scene cube at world (16.9, 9-13) NOT in our spawn list) adds an unaccounted ~4 m wall right across the WP2→WP3 corridor.  At 1.5 m inflation the cube + walls + couch form a near-solid barrier; at 1.0 m the gap pillar_01↔wall opens to 3 m, wall↔cube opens to ~1.5 m.  Drone is 0.6 m wide so 1.0 m halo on 0.5 m obstacle radius gives 0.5 m per-side clearance — tight but workable with the avoider as backup repulsion (repulsive_gain doubled to 6.0 to compensate).
occupancy_grid._comment_max_static_cells: Issue #645 — was 500, capping the static layer at exactly the budget meant new voxel promotions were silently dropped once the outbound wake filled the cap.  0 = unlimited; with the Phase 3 instance-promotion gate (#642) blocking noise voxels at the source, raw cell-count is bounded by real obstacle volume rather than noise accumulation, so removing the cap is safe.  Empirical: drone collisions dropped 461→112 with all #645 fixes active but `Max promoted = 500` (= cap) — the cap is binding.  Lift it and see if the perception pipeline can keep up.

voxel_input._comment: PATH A voxel subscriber enabled — MaskDepthProjector batches from P2 feed the grid via the instance-promotion gate.
voxel_input._comment_static_cell_ttl_s: Issue #698 — tightened from 30 s to 5 s.  Run 2026-05-02_161132_ABORTED showed the wake from the outbound leg accumulated to 4596 ghost static cells in ~20 s and walled off WP2.  At 30 s TTL the wake outlives the entire scenario; at 5 s, an unobserved cell decays before the drone is past it.  Real obstacles get re-observed every frame so they stay fresh.  This is a stop-gap before the SAM-aware TTL refinement (planned next) where voxels grouped under the same SAM instance share a longer instance-level TTL — that lets coherent semantic objects persist while orphan/noise voxels age out fast.  Original #645 30 s setting commentary preserved in git history.
voxel_input._comment_instance_gate: Issue #638 Phase 4 — instance-aware promotion. PATH A voxels carry instance_id from Phase 1's clusterer + Phase 2's tracker. Voxels with id=0 (noise — single-frame depth artefacts that didn't form a coherent cluster) are rejected outright. Tracked instances must promote through TWO gates: (a) tracker-level minimum observation count (today: 3 frames, see voxel_promotion_hits in planner_cfg) and (b) voxel_input.instance_promotion_observations below (today: 8 frames). The voxel_input gate is the authoritative one for grid promotion — see _comment_instance_promotion_observations for tuning rationale. Real obstacles cluster across frames and promote; transient noise never accumulates. This is the architectural fix that PR #636's empirical journey proved was needed (see PR #639/#640/#641).
voxel_input._comment_position_clamp_m: Issue #645 — was 200 m (far too generous; 6.2 M voxels with median X=42 m, p90=43 m of pure depth-blowup ghosts).  Tightened to 30 m on 2026-04-30 (mission X∈[0,25], so 30 gave 5 m headroom).  Run 2026-05-02_152123: drone wandered to X=35 because the planner's snap-lateral fallback drifted WP3 from (25,12) to (34,26) — once past X=30, EVERY new voxel was clamped out (inserted=0 dropped=10240/tick), planner went blind, drone hung in front of TemplateCube_Rounded_62 at (35,4) with 20+ micro-collisions.  Bumped to 50 m so the voxels stay visible if the drone overshoots into 25-50 m territory.  Trade-off: re-admits some depth-blowup ghosts at 30-50 m but the #660 min_confidence=0.7 + #659 mask-size + #658 altitude filters together drop the bulk.  Real fix is bounding the snap-lateral offset (Bug 1 from triage) so the drone doesn't wander past WP area in the first place — separate PR.
voxel_input._comment_min_confidence: Issue #645 — was 0.3 (lenient).  Trace analysis on run 2026-04-30_182519: voxel_on_target reached 22 % (passing the threshold) but 3.8 M raw voxels accumulated over the run produced ~3 M off-target ghosts at obstacle altitude (#658 sky/ground filter clipped sky/ground but the residual mid-altitude depth-noise voxels remained).  Tighten to 0.7 — DA V2 reports high confidence on real-surface pixels; depth-noise voxels typically have confidence < 0.5.  Drops the bulk of remaining ghosts at the grid-insert boundary.  Real obstacles re-observed every frame stay confident → keep their static cells.
voxel_input._comment_instance_promotion_observations: Issue #645 — was 5 (≈0.5 s at 10 Hz).  Bump to 8 (≈0.8 s) for additional noise rejection.  Real obstacles in scenario 33 are stationary so ANY observation count works for promotion; transient noise instances flicker for 1-3 frames and never reach 8.  Cost: 0.3 s extra latency before a new real obstacle promotes — acceptable at 2 m/s cruise (drone covers ~0.6 m in that time, well within the 1.5 m inflation halo).
voxel_input._comment_allow_radar_promotion: Issue #645 — radar-confirmed detected objects bypass the voxel-input promotion pause and reach the strategic path planner.  Without this, radar tracks only feed ObstacleAvoider3D (reactive deflection) — D* Lite was blind to radar-detected obstacles.  Radar gives accurate range from the first hit (no convergence latency / ghost cells), so it's the safe subset of camera+radar fusion to allow through while voxel_input is the primary static-cell source.

occupancy_grid._comment_cross_veto: Issue #698 Fix #1 — late-stage cross-modal veto on PATH A static promotion (ADR-013 §2 item 4).  Cells <short_range_m promote on stereo alone; cells >=short_range_m in radar FOV require radar agreement; cells >=short_range_m outside FOV stay dynamic forever (with FOV-residency / dynamic-age-cap escape hatches).  Default policy values come from the research note in #698 — short_range=10m crossover where radar wins range accuracy, 100ms staleness gate aligned to radar's ~20Hz tick, 1m gate-min-radius growing as 0.25R+0.1 with range, 2s positive-silence threshold, 30s age cap.
occupancy_grid._comment_short_range_workaround: Issue #702 — Cosys-AirSim's lidar-emulated radar misses 5/7 spawned obstacles in this scenario regardless of cluster cap.  Per-object recall analyzer (tools/diag/per_object_recall.py) on run 2026-05-03_134342_ABORTED: spawned-obstacle radar recall = 14% (1/7), voxel recall = 71% (5/7) with sub-meter accuracy.  ADR-013's short_range=10m was tuned for real AWR1843 radar (cm-class range past 10m); Cosys radar is unreliable at all ranges in this scene.  Bumped to 30m so the camera has sole promotion authority within the obstacle field.  The cross-veto only applies past 30m where camera depth is unreliable anyway.  REVERT to 10m once the underlying radar accuracy is fixed (see #702).

mission_planner._comment_static_obstacles: Issue #698 — populated from tools/diag/blocks_default_inventory.json after we discovered (run 2026-05-04_224726) that camera-only perception only sees the SOUTH FACE of the 20×20 m cube (13 static cells promoted, planner thinks the cube interior is free space, plans a straight line through the wall).  Each entry is one of the 4 cube footprints (10×10 m, 3-cube stack vertically) plus the 2 BP_PIPCamera clusters at (~6, 14).  radius_m=5.25 = (10 m / 2) × 1.05 to inscribe the full square footprint with 5% margin; combined with the 1.5 m planner inflation each footprint has ~6.75 m forbidden halo, the 4 cube halos overlap to form one solid 20×20 m blob (correct).  This is Option A from issue #703 follow-up: HD-map populated from sim ground truth so the planner has full obstacle knowledge, isolating planner+avoider behaviour from perception completeness.  REVERT this list to validate the perception-discovers-obstacles path (the real-hardware contract).
mission_planner._comment_waypoints:
Issue #698 — waypoints redesigned 2026-05-04 after the live scene inventory (tools/diag/blocks_default_inventory.json) revealed the 'cube field' is actually ONE solid 20×20×15m block (12 stacked TC sub-cubes touching at N=17-37, E=4-24, Z=-1..14) plus a small BP_PIPCamera cluster at (~6.5, ~13.5, ~5).  The previous waypoint design assumed 4 separate strips with corridors between them — that was based on a wrong DEFAULT_CUBES table mined from collision logs.  Old WP3 (30, 14) was LITERALLY INSIDE the solid block, making the mission geometrically impossible — drone correctly stopped at the south face every run.

New route forces real obstacle avoidance without putting any WP inside an obstacle:
  WP1 (0, 0)   — start
  WP2 (12, 14) — 5 m south of block, 6 m north of BP_PIPCamera cluster
  WP3 (40, 14) — 3 m PAST the block's north edge (N=37); planner MUST detour east or west around the wall
  WP4 (40, 30) — east leg, 6 m clear of block east edge (E=24)
  WP5 (10, 30) — west leg along N=30, north of block, clear
  WP6 (0, 0)   — return home

Clearances: every WP ≥ 3 m from any cube.  Detour distance WP2→WP3: ~30 m via west side of block (E≤2 corridor) or ~30 m via east side (E≥27 corridor).

waypoints._comment: South-of-block approach. 5 m south of block (N=17), 6 m north of BP_PIPCamera cluster (N=6.5). Bumped from old (10,14) to (12,14) for camera-cluster clearance.
Past north edge of block (N=37) by 3 m. Planner MUST find a detour route around the 20 m wall — east via E≥27 m corridor or west via E≤2 m corridor. This is the real obstacle-avoidance test.
NE leg along N=40, well past block east edge (E=24) by 6 m. Clear straight-line leg from WP3.
West leg along N=30, north of block (N=27 edge). Clear east-to-west traverse.

pass_criteria._comment: Issue #698 — re-scoped 2026-05-04 from 'PATH A perception stress' to 'PLANNER + AVOIDER with HD-map + ground-truth perception'.  Pass = mission completed all 6 waypoints, RTL → LAND, zero obstacle collisions, all three Cosys ground-truth perception backends initialised, HD-map obstacles loaded.  The previous PATH A pipeline checks (MaskProj.Published, VoxelSub firing) belong in the next scenario (perception-only test) — they would mask real planner failures here because PATH A may legitimately publish 0 batches when the HD-map is the sole obstacle source.
pass_criteria._comment_collisions: Collision #1 with 'Ground' is the unavoidable startup-touch from spawning the drone at z=0 (penetration=0) — every Cosys run produces it.  We allow that one and only fail on collision #2+ (which can only happen if the drone hit a real obstacle).  Listing each obstacle name family explicitly (TemplateCube/BP_/Pillar/Wall/Cube) so a typo in one doesn't silently let collisions slip through.

checks.name: Ground-truth radar backend + first scan
checks._comment: Issue #698 — radar.backend=cosys_airsim_groundtruth active and emitting detections.

checks.name: HD-map static obstacles loaded
checks._comment: All 6 static_obstacles entries from blocks_default_inventory.json loaded into the grid.

checks.name: D* Lite computed valid path
checks._comment: Planner produced at least one valid path including the WP3 detour.

checks.name: Mission completed (RTL → LAND)
checks._comment: Drone returned home and transitioned to LAND.

checks.name: VIO remained NOMINAL
checks._comment: Inverse check: zero DEGRADED events (Cosys ground-truth VIO should stay green).

checks.name: Zero obstacle collisions
checks._comment: Inverse check: only the unavoidable startup ground-touch (collision #1) is allowed; any collision #2+ is a real obstacle hit.
